title: salvia candidissima subsp. candidissima
intro: Salvia candidissima subsp. candidissima, a rare and little-known species of the Salvia genus, is a type of flowering plant native to South Africa, characterized by its striking white flowers and blue-grey foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Salvia candidissima subsp. candidissima are said to include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and providing antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, or as a tincture to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
